skill.name: wiki-changelog
skill.description: Analyzes git commit history and generates structured changelogs categorized by change type.
skill.summary: Turn recent git history into concise, grouped changelog entries.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported three high-severity weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md, but manual review found only natural language instructions. Lines 3, 20, and 25 describe changelog generation and commit grouping, with no code, cryptographic implementation, network behavior, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged SKILL.md lines contain documentation text about analyzing git history, generating descriptions, and merging related commits. No weak cryptographic algorithm, executable code, or security-sensitive implementation was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the pattern matches are plain prose, not code. The file contains only skill instructions and no cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Repository changes can be hard to explain from raw commit history. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to group commits into clear changelog sections.
actual_capabilities: Examines git commit history for recent repository activity.
Groups changes by daily and weekly time periods.
Classifies commits into features, fixes, refactoring, docs, config, dependencies, and breaking changes.
Creates concise user-facing descriptions from commit messages.
Highlights breaking changes and asks for migration notes when needed.
limitations: It depends on available git history and commit message quality.
It does not run a release process or publish documentation by itself.
It may need README context to use exact project terminology.
It cannot verify runtime behavior from commits alone.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Weekly Project Update
use_cases.description: Summarize the last week of commits into sections that stakeholders can scan quickly.

use_cases.title: Draft Release Notes
use_cases.description: Convert recent development work into categorized notes before a version release.

use_cases.title: Document Repository Activity
use_cases.description: Create a wiki-ready record of changes for maintainers and contributors.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Recent Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a changelog for the recent commits in this repository.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple changelog.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Weekly Changelog
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the git history and create a weekly changelog grouped by change type.
prompt_templates.scenario: Regular documentation update for active development.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Release Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate release notes from commits since the last release tag. Highlight fixes, features, dependencies, and breaking changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Wiki Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze recent commits, merge related work into coherent entries, and write a wiki changelog using terminology from the README.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation output with project-specific wording.

output_examples.input: Generate a changelog for the last seven days.
output_examples.output: A dated changelog grouped into features, fixes, documentation, configuration, and dependency updates.

output_examples.input: Summarize commits since the last release.
output_examples.output: Release notes that combine related commits, describe user-facing impact, and call out breaking changes.

output_examples.input: What changed recently in this repository?
output_examples.output: A concise activity summary organized by time period and change category.

best_practices: Ask for a specific time window, tag range, or branch before generating final notes.
Provide README or product context when project terminology matters.
Review breaking change entries before publishing them to users.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every commit message as a final user-facing note.
Do not hide breaking changes inside general feature sections.
Do not publish changelogs without checking generated summaries against the repository context.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates structured changelogs from git commit history.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it modify repository files?
faq.answer: The skill describes changelog generation only. It does not include file editing instructions.

faq.question: Can it detect breaking changes?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to highlight breaking changes and include migration notes.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in SKILL.md.

faq.question: What input improves the results?
faq.answer: A clear commit range, release target, and README context improve the changelog quality.
